fundamentals.name: Robinhood Markets, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Robinhood Markets, Inc. operates financial services platform in the United States. The company's platform allows users to invest in stocks, exchange-traded funds (ETFs), and American depository receipts. It offers fractional trading, recurring investments, access to investing on margin, fully-paid securities lending, cash sweep, instant withdrawals, retirement program, around-the-clock trading, joint investing accounts, event contracts, future contract services, and short selling. The company also provides various learning and education solutions comprise Snacks, an accessible digest of business news stories for a new generation of investors; Learn, which is an online collection of guides, feature tutorials, and financial dictionary; Newsfeeds that offer access to free, premium news from sites from various sites, such as Barron's, Reuters, and Dow Jones. In addition, the company offers In-App Education, a resource that covers investing fundamentals, including why people invest, a stock market overview, and tips on how to define investing goals, as well as allows customers to understand the basics of investing before their first trade; and Crypto Learn and Earn, an educational module available to various crypto customers through Robinhood Learn to teach customers the basics related to cryptocurrency. Further, it provides Robinhood credit cards, cash card and spending accounts, and wallets. Robinhood Markets, Inc. was incorporated in 2013 and is headquartered in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: Profitable growth company with strong margins but facing slowing earnings and high valuation multiples.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimate by 2.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-29.
positives: Gross margin 92.2% and Profit margin 41.1% indicate strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity 21.5% shows effective use of shareholder investments.
Forward P/E 27.64x is lower than trailing P/E 36.68x, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $98.40.
negatives: Revenue growth 15.1% YoY is solid, but earnings growth is only 2.7% YoY.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (-4.5%) and fiscal year (-8.4%) are negative.
Debt/Equity ratio of 140.48 is high, indicating significant leverage.
Free cash flow (TTM) is not available, making it difficult to assess cash generation.

news.paragraph: Robinhood is opening its platform to allow AI agents to trade stocks and make credit card purchases, a move that could significantly expand user engagement and transaction volume. This innovative step integrates artificial intelligence directly into the user experience, potentially attracting new demographics and increasing platform utility. While the immediate impact on revenue is unclear, the strategy positions Robinhood at the forefront of AI-driven financial services.

headlines.title: Robinhood Opens Platform to AI Agents for Stock Trading and Credit Card Spending
Robinhood Will Allow Users’ AI Agents To Trade Stocks
Robinhood Opens Platform to AI Agents for Trading
Your AI agent can now trade for you on Robinhood. And buy stuff with your credit card too
Robinhood Lets Customers Use AI to Trade Stocks, Make Credit-Card Purchases

examples.body: $HOOD I doubt i will hand over the keys to my portfolio to AI but i might allocate like 1K and let it go nuts. id like to paper trade w the AI for a bit first …
$HOOD needs to gap up from here to have any meaning
